jokes.text: That's shelley long.
jokes.setup: Character comments that you can see the craziness in the woman's eyes from the photo
jokes.punchline: Reveals the photo is actually of actress Shelley Long, not the alleged woman
jokes.explanation: Perfect comedic timing and misdirection. The setup makes us believe they're looking at the accuser's photo, then the deadpan reveal undercuts everything. Economical and well-structured.

dialogue.speaker: Larry
dialogue.line: That's shelley long.

jokes.text: You know, I am prepared to publicly allege that Larry fondles my buttocks whenever I go in to pitch jokes.
jokes.setup: Discussion of the tabloid allegations against Larry
jokes.punchline: Writer sarcastically suggests making their own false allegation for money
jokes.explanation: Solid meta-commentary on how tabloid culture works. Good structure but not particularly original or memorable.

dialogue.speaker: Writer
dialogue.line: You know, I am prepared to publicly allege that Larry fondles my buttocks whenever I go in to pitch jokes.

jokes.text: Million, million and 5, depending upon if you can prove your buttocks is a source of income. Not since college.
jokes.setup: Discussion of how much the buttock fondling allegation would be worth
jokes.punchline: Admission that buttocks hasn't been income source since college
jokes.explanation: Functional follow-up joke with mild TMI humor. Gets the job done but nothing special.

dialogue.speaker: Other Writer
dialogue.line: Million, million and 5, depending upon if you can prove your buttocks is a source of income.

dialogue.speaker: First Writer
dialogue.line: Not since college.

jokes.text: No. Baywatch was renewed.
jokes.setup: Larry asks if there's anything good in the paper
jokes.punchline: Paula delivers the news about Baywatch renewal as if it's bad news
jokes.explanation: Sharp industry insider humor. The deadpan delivery treats lowbrow TV success as inherently depressing news.

dialogue.speaker: Paula
dialogue.line: No. Baywatch was renewed.

jokes.text: Well, thanks for lying through your teeth. I appreciate it.
jokes.setup: Staff claims they haven't seen the article about Larry's alleged affair
jokes.punchline: Larry sarcastically thanks them for the obvious lie
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Larry's paranoia and awareness of office dynamics.

dialogue.speaker: Larry
dialogue.line: Well, thanks for lying through your teeth. I appreciate it.

jokes.text: His name guy? No. He's just a guy.
jokes.setup: Larry asks if someone's name is Guy
jokes.punchline: Artie clarifies it's not a name, just describing him as 'a guy'
jokes.explanation: Simple wordplay confusion. Functional but predictable.

dialogue.speaker: Larry
dialogue.line: His name guy?

dialogue.speaker: Artie
dialogue.line: No. He's just a guy.

jokes.text: I did not sleep with that woman from montana. No? No. Just checking.
jokes.setup: Larry emphatically denies sleeping with the woman
jokes.punchline: Artie's casual 'just checking' response
jokes.explanation: Excellent character work. Artie's casual response undercuts Larry's dramatic denial perfectly.

dialogue.speaker: Larry
dialogue.line: I did not sleep with that woman from montana. No? No.

dialogue.speaker: Artie
dialogue.line: Just checking.

jokes.text: Hey, I get some on a nightly basis. I'm still excited about mimi rogers.
jokes.setup: Larry says he's excited about Mimi Rogers because he hasn't met anyone he likes
jokes.punchline: Hank brags he gets action nightly but is still excited about her
jokes.explanation: Classic Hank boasting that reveals his desperation. Good character consistency.

dialogue.speaker: Hank
dialogue.line: Hey, I get some on a nightly basis. I'm still excited about mimi rogers.

jokes.text: Oh, that would explain the lobster. How's that? Maine. Lobster. I got it. You're working on a bit. I'll play along. Maine lobsters...
jokes.setup: Hank mentions his tie was given by Maine Chamber of Commerce
jokes.punchline: Larry makes obvious Maine-lobster connection, Hank thinks it's a comedy bit
jokes.explanation: Perfect Hank moment - he's so desperate to be part of comedy that he sees bits where none exist.

dialogue.speaker: Larry
dialogue.line: Oh, that would explain the lobster.

dialogue.speaker: Hank
dialogue.line: How's that?

dialogue.speaker: Larry
dialogue.line: Maine. Lobster.

dialogue.speaker: Hank
dialogue.line: I got it. You're working on a bit. I'll play along. Maine lobsters...

jokes.text: Well, that usually happens on Tuesday mornings, doesn't it? That's why it threw you.
jokes.setup: Hank complains about garbage dumped on his porch on Monday
jokes.punchline: Larry suggests the timing is what's wrong, not the garbage itself
jokes.explanation: Smart misdirection. Makes it sound like getting garbage dumped is normal, just on the wrong day.

dialogue.speaker: Larry
dialogue.line: Well, that usually happens on Tuesday mornings, doesn't it? That's why it threw you.

jokes.text: I once had a woman, and, uh... do we have time for this? I don't think we need to get into the details of this. She followed me across 7 states.
jokes.setup: Hank starts to tell a story about a woman
jokes.punchline: Despite Larry trying to stop him, he reveals she followed him across 7 states
jokes.explanation: Good escalation and character work. Hank ignores social cues and makes things worse.

dialogue.speaker: Hank
dialogue.line: I once had a woman, and, uh... do we have time for this? I don't think we need to get into the details of this. She followed me across 7 states.

jokes.text: Her name was Cindy halloran. Cindy halloran from pensacola, thank you very much.
jokes.setup: Larry tries to end the story by saying some things are best left to imagination
jokes.punchline: Hank provides full name and location despite being told to stop
jokes.explanation: Excellent escalation. Hank completely ignores the attempt to shut him up and provides even more problematic detail.

dialogue.speaker: Hank
dialogue.line: Her name was Cindy halloran. Cindy halloran from pensacola, thank you very much.

jokes.text: People are going to think I called her Cindy fuckpot or something. From the philadelphia fuckpots.
jokes.setup: Artie explains they bleeped Hank's mention of the woman's last name
jokes.punchline: Hank imagines audience will think he said something much worse
jokes.explanation: Funny escalation of Hank's paranoia. 'Philadelphia fuckpots' is absurd and quotable.

dialogue.speaker: Hank
dialogue.line: People are going to think I called her Cindy fuckpot or something. From the philadelphia fuckpots.

jokes.text: OK, I happened to say the word cum. So what? In front of Jessica tandy. Oh, that crybaby. She's heard all this shit before.
jokes.setup: Discussion of Hank's previous on-air bleeped moment
jokes.punchline: Hank dismisses Jessica Tandy as a crybaby who's heard worse
jokes.explanation: Great character moment. Hank's complete lack of awareness about propriety with a respected elderly actress.

dialogue.speaker: Hank
dialogue.line: OK, I happened to say the word cum. So what? In front of Jessica tandy. Oh, that crybaby. She's heard all this shit before.

jokes.text: No, well, you don't become nude. He didn't take his clothes off. You become nude. I've heard that about you. No, I don't become... no. What you're thinking is that I'm very becoming nude.
jokes.setup: Discussion about someone not becoming nude in a movie
jokes.punchline: Larry tries to turn 'becoming nude' into 'very becoming nude'
jokes.explanation: Weak wordplay that feels forced and unfunny. Larry trying too hard to be clever.

dialogue.speaker: Larry
dialogue.line: No, well, you don't become nude. He didn't take his clothes off. You become nude. I've heard that about you. No, I don't become... no. What you're thinking is that I'm very becoming nude.

jokes.text: Would you like a piece of gum? Oh, just half. Do you wanna go out sometime?
jokes.setup: Larry offers gum, Mimi asks for half
jokes.punchline: Larry immediately segues to asking her out
jokes.explanation: Good character beat showing Larry's awkward directness and desperation.

dialogue.speaker: Larry
dialogue.line: Would you like a piece of gum?

dialogue.speaker: Mimi
dialogue.line: Oh, just half.

dialogue.speaker: Larry
dialogue.line: Do you wanna go out sometime?

jokes.text: You don't waste any time. Well, they're only on for 3½ minutes, so I thought I'd get right to it.
jokes.setup: Mimi comments on Larry's directness in asking her out
jokes.punchline: Larry explains the time constraint of the show segment
jokes.explanation: Excellent meta-joke about the artificial constraints of TV that forces Larry to rush romance. Sharp and economical.

dialogue.speaker: Mimi
dialogue.line: You don't waste any time.

dialogue.speaker: Larry
dialogue.line: Well, they're only on for 3½ minutes, so I thought I'd get right to it.

jokes.text: Boy, do I have a big surprise for you, Mr. Larry Sanders.
jokes.setup: Hank announces he has a surprise for Larry
jokes.punchline: The anticipation itself is comedic given Hank's track record
jokes.explanation: Sets up the disaster to come. Works because we know Hank's surprises are always terrible.

dialogue.speaker: Hank
dialogue.line: Boy, do I have a big surprise for you, Mr. Larry Sanders.

jokes.text: This is not my cousin. I'm Mary Beth nagler from montana. I'm having Larry's baby.
jokes.setup: Hank introduces what he thinks is Larry's cousin
jokes.punchline: The woman reveals she's the pregnant accuser from the news story
jokes.explanation: Excellent escalation and plot convergence. The setup makes this moment explosive - Hank's incompetence creates maximum disaster.

dialogue.speaker: Mary Beth
dialogue.line: This is not my cousin. I'm Mary Beth nagler from montana. I'm having Larry's baby.

jokes.text: Gimme the button.
jokes.setup: The massive embarrassment of the pregnant woman appearing on stage
jokes.punchline: Larry's only response is to demand the 'Hey Now' button back
jokes.explanation: Perfect character moment. In crisis, Larry focuses on petty control. The economy is perfect - two words capture everything.

dialogue.speaker: Larry
dialogue.line: Gimme the button.

jokes.text: Young lady, am I to understand you are blackmailing Mr. Sanders?
jokes.setup: Woman mentions Hard Copy is offering $5,000 for her story
jokes.punchline: Hank formally accuses her of blackmail on live TV
jokes.explanation: Good Hank moment - trying to be the hero but making things worse with his formal, inappropriate response.

dialogue.speaker: Hank
dialogue.line: Young lady, am I to understand you are blackmailing Mr. Sanders?

jokes.text: Move another inch toward Larry, and I'll take you down hard.
jokes.setup: Tense confrontation on stage
jokes.punchline: Artie threatens physical violence against a pregnant woman on live TV
jokes.explanation: Great escalation. Artie's protective instincts lead to completely inappropriate TV moment.

dialogue.speaker: Artie
dialogue.line: Move another inch toward Larry, and I'll take you down hard.

jokes.text: Wonderful, Hank. Are you insane? Yes, I think I'm insane.
jokes.setup: Larry sarcastically asks if Hank is insane after the disaster
jokes.punchline: Hank sincerely agrees that he might be
jokes.explanation: Perfect Hank response. Instead of defending himself, he considers the possibility seriously.

dialogue.speaker: Larry
dialogue.line: Wonderful, Hank. Are you insane?

dialogue.speaker: Hank
dialogue.line: Yes, I think I'm insane.

jokes.text: Is she the only one, or did you run across some muslim extremist who'd like to meet Larry?
jokes.setup: Asking if the pregnant woman was the only problematic audience member Hank found
jokes.punchline: Suggests even worse security threats Hank might have invited
jokes.explanation: Dark escalation that's very much in Artie's character. Shows the scope of potential disasters Hank could cause.

dialogue.speaker: Artie
dialogue.line: Is she the only one, or did you run across some muslim extremist who'd like to meet Larry?

jokes.text: Otherwise, you end up with, what we call in our business a crap column.
jokes.setup: Hank telling reporter not to fill column with crap about Larry
jokes.punchline: His pompous explanation of industry terminology
jokes.explanation: Classic Hank - trying to sound professional while being completely unprofessional.

dialogue.speaker: Hank
dialogue.line: Otherwise, you end up with, what we call in our business a crap column.

jokes.text: I was, uh, personally abused as a child. No, not sexually. Physically. I was beaten, and I was kicked. You know, that sort of thing. Well, who do you think did it? The other kids, that's who.
jokes.setup: Hank offers the reporter a story about childhood abuse
jokes.punchline: Reveals it was just normal playground bullying, not family abuse
jokes.explanation: Excellent misdirection. Sets up expectation of serious abuse story, reveals mundane childhood bullying. Perfect Hank delusion.

dialogue.speaker: Hank
dialogue.line: I was, uh, personally abused as a child. No, not sexually. Physically. I was beaten, and I was kicked. You know, that sort of thing. Well, who do you think did it? The other kids, that's who.

jokes.text: What, you can't get pregnant from saying hello unless you say it to warren beatty.
jokes.setup: Larry explaining he may have just said hello to the woman
jokes.punchline: Warren Beatty's reputation for seducing women
jokes.explanation: Sharp Hollywood insider joke. Good structure and timing, very quotable.

dialogue.speaker: Larry
dialogue.line: What, you can't get pregnant from saying hello unless you say it to warren beatty.

jokes.text: Me and Tom snyder... shoving match right there in the thrifty parking lot. You're right. You're right, that didn't happen. Roseanne didn't marry her assistant, either. She gets to make up this stuff. Why the hell can't I?
jokes.setup: Artie starts to tell a story about Tom Snyder
jokes.punchline: Admits he made it up and questions why he can't lie like others do
jokes.explanation: Great character moment showing Artie's frustration with Hollywood BS and his own moral standards.

dialogue.speaker: Artie
dialogue.line: Me and Tom snyder... shoving match right there in the thrifty parking lot. You're right. You're right, that didn't happen. Roseanne didn't marry her assistant, either. She gets to make up this stuff. Why the hell can't I?

jokes.text: I'm on the one-phay.
jokes.setup: Hank is on the phone
jokes.punchline: He uses fake pig latin to sound sophisticated
jokes.explanation: Pure Hank - trying to sound clever but being ridiculous. Character-specific but not particularly funny.

dialogue.speaker: Hank
dialogue.line: I'm on the one-phay.

jokes.text: Red wine gives me hives.
jokes.setup: Hank offering the reporter potential stories about himself
jokes.punchline: Mundane personal detail presented as newsworthy
jokes.explanation: Good character work. Hank's desperation to be newsworthy leads to pathetic oversharing.

dialogue.speaker: Hank
dialogue.line: Red wine gives me hives.

jokes.text: I got a special thing for, uh, asian ladies.
jokes.setup: Hank still trying to find interesting story
jokes.punchline: Inappropriate personal revelation
jokes.explanation: Cringe humor that fits Hank's character - inappropriate oversharing in his desperation for attention.

dialogue.speaker: Hank
dialogue.line: I got a special thing for, uh, asian ladies.

jokes.text: I got a special thing for, uh, asian ladies.
jokes.setup: Hank continuing to offer story ideas to reporter
jokes.punchline: Inappropriate racial preference revealed as potential news
jokes.explanation: Uncomfortable character moment. Sets up later payoff when Asian women actually appear.

dialogue.speaker: Hank
dialogue.line: I got a special thing for, uh, asian ladies.

jokes.text: Normally, you can only see to whitney houston's house, but it must be very clear tonight.
jokes.setup: Larry showing off his ability to see Kevin Costner's house through telescope
jokes.punchline: Casual mention that he regularly spies on Whitney Houston
jokes.explanation: Good reveal of Larry's creepy celebrity stalking behavior presented as casual hobby.

dialogue.speaker: Larry
dialogue.line: Normally, you can only see to whitney houston's house, but it must be very clear tonight.

jokes.text: If that's Kevin costner, make sure that we pretend we were just looking at the moon, OK?
jokes.setup: Someone's at the door while Larry is using telescope
jokes.punchline: Larry's paranoid plan to hide his celebrity stalking
jokes.explanation: Perfect Larry paranoia. His immediate assumption is that he'll be caught and need a cover story.

dialogue.speaker: Larry
dialogue.line: If that's Kevin costner, make sure that we pretend we were just looking at the moon, OK?

jokes.text: I loved you in star wars. Oh. I think you're confusing me with Carrie Fisher.
jokes.setup: Mary Beth recognizes Mimi Rogers
jokes.punchline: Confuses her with completely different actress
jokes.explanation: Standard mistaken celebrity identity bit. Functional but predictable.

dialogue.speaker: Mary Beth
dialogue.line: I loved you in star wars.

dialogue.speaker: Mimi
dialogue.line: Oh. I think you're confusing me with Carrie Fisher.

jokes.text: So did I, by the way.
jokes.setup: Mary Beth says she did Princess Leia buns after seeing Star Wars
jokes.punchline: Larry admits he did the same hairstyle
jokes.explanation: Good character moment. Larry's inappropriate sharing trying to connect with his accuser.

dialogue.speaker: Larry
dialogue.line: So did I, by the way.

jokes.text: She says, 'one large birthmark with 2 smaller ones above it at the base of the penis forming a mickey mouse head.' That is not my penis.
jokes.setup: Lawyer explaining the woman can describe Larry's distinguishing marks
jokes.punchline: Specific, embarrassing description followed by Larry's weak denial
jokes.explanation: Brilliant escalation. The specific Disney reference makes it both absurd and memorable. Larry's denial is perfectly weak.

dialogue.speaker: Lloyd
dialogue.line: She says, 'one large birthmark with 2 smaller ones above it at the base of the penis forming a mickey mouse head.'

dialogue.speaker: Larry
dialogue.line: That is not my penis.

jokes.text: Jesus, Larry, she knows all about little mickey.
jokes.setup: The Mickey Mouse birthmark revelation
jokes.punchline: Artie gives Larry's penis a Disney nickname
jokes.explanation: Perfect callback that creates a recurring character name. 'Little Mickey' is instantly quotable and character-specific to Artie.

dialogue.speaker: Artie
dialogue.line: Jesus, Larry, she knows all about little mickey.

jokes.text: No, she was one of the top people in the space program. Yeah, she was a waitress, Artie.
jokes.setup: Artie asks if the woman was a waitress
jokes.punchline: Larry's sarcastic response before confirming the obvious
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Larry's defensive sarcasm when caught in embarrassing situation.

dialogue.speaker: Larry
dialogue.line: No, she was one of the top people in the space program. Yeah, she was a waitress, Artie.

jokes.text: She gave me a handjob in the parking lot at Denny's.
jokes.setup: Building tension about what actually happened with the woman
jokes.punchline: The pathetic, specific truth of the encounter
jokes.explanation: Perfect culmination of the mystery. The specificity of 'Denny's' makes it both pathetic and real. Great character revelation.

dialogue.speaker: Larry
dialogue.line: She gave me a handjob in the parking lot at Denny's.

jokes.text: There's nothing wrong with a handjob, Larry. In my day, I've had plenty of 'em in far worse places than a parking lot. I remember one time I was visiting St. Patrick's cathedral. It was a Thursday. There weren't many people in the pews in the back.
jokes.setup: Artie trying to console Larry about handjob
jokes.punchline: Admits to sexual activity in church with specific details
jokes.explanation: Brilliant Artie escalation that makes Larry's situation seem tame by comparison. The specific details (Thursday, back pews) make it funnier and more believable.

dialogue.speaker: Artie
dialogue.line: There's nothing wrong with a handjob, Larry. In my day, I've had plenty of 'em in far worse places than a parking lot. I remember one time I was visiting St. Patrick's cathedral. It was a Thursday. There weren't many people in the pews in the back.

jokes.text: There's nothing wrong with a handjob, Larry. In my day, I've had plenty of 'em in far worse places than a parking lot. I remember one time I was visiting St. Patrick's cathedral. It was a Thursday.
jokes.setup: Larry feels ashamed about the parking lot handjob
jokes.punchline: Artie tops it with a church story, adding unnecessary detail about the day
jokes.explanation: Brilliant escalation and character work. Artie's casual sacrilege and the Thursday detail make it perfect. Only Artie could deliver this.

dialogue.speaker: Artie
dialogue.line: There's nothing wrong with a handjob, Larry. In my day, I've had plenty of 'em in far worse places than a parking lot. I remember one time I was visiting St. Patrick's cathedral. It was a Thursday.

jokes.text: I know the television industry has more than its share of pathetic types, but Kingsley has taken the prize.
jokes.setup: Newspaper critic reviewing Hank's bizarre phone call
jokes.punchline: Declares Hank the most pathetic person in television
jokes.explanation: Good callback to Hank's desperate phone call with professional critical assessment. The 'taken the prize' phrasing is perfect.

dialogue.line: I know the television industry has more than its share of pathetic types, but Kingsley has taken the prize.

jokes.text: I didn't say anything about Denny's. I think you did. No, I didn't, did I? Yeah, yeah. I heard Denny's.
jokes.setup: Lloyd mentions Denny's in the handjob story
jokes.punchline: Larry realizes he never told them that detail, exposing his lie
jokes.explanation: Excellent plot mechanics. Larry's caught in his own lie through a detail he didn't realize he'd revealed.

dialogue.speaker: Larry
dialogue.line: I didn't say anything about Denny's. I think you did.

dialogue.speaker: Lloyd
dialogue.line: No, I didn't, did I?

dialogue.speaker: Artie
dialogue.line: Yeah, yeah. I heard Denny's.

jokes.text: Hank, Cindy halloran from pensacola. I heard you mention me on the show.
jokes.setup: Hank mentioned this name earlier and was bleeped
jokes.punchline: The actual person shows up because she heard her name
jokes.explanation: Perfect callback payoff. The consequences of Hank's earlier mistake come full circle. Great escalation.

dialogue.speaker: Cindy Halloran
dialogue.line: Hank, Cindy halloran from pensacola. I heard you mention me on the show.

jokes.text: Call security. Call security now.
jokes.setup: Hank realizes he's in trouble with Cindy Halloran
jokes.punchline: Immediate panic and security request
jokes.explanation: Good payoff showing Hank's immediate recognition of the consequences of his actions. Perfect character panic response.

dialogue.speaker: Hank
dialogue.line: Call security. Call security now.

jokes.text: why don't I, uh... just come by, and we'll sit in the car, you know, and, uh... talk.
jokes.setup: Larry trying to ask Mimi out again despite everything that's happened
jokes.punchline: He unconsciously references the exact scenario that got him in trouble
jokes.explanation: Perfect character moment. Larry learns nothing and suggests the exact same scenario that created his problems. Great circular ending.

dialogue.speaker: Larry
dialogue.line: why don't I, uh... just come by, and we'll sit in the car, you know, and, uh... talk.
